text_content: All software and FPGA versions, and the PCB part identifiers are fixed for a certain build, while the PCB version (dash number and revision) can vary between system to system. A subsystem is responsible for checking the compatibility of itself and its software and FPGA components. It is also responsible for checking the compatibility between its components, such as FPGA to PCB or PCB to PCB. Supervisor specifies a list of compatible versions for each Node in its configuration data item tree. It compares the Node version received from the Node with the compatibility list and raises an active fault if the received version is not found in the list. A Node does not cross check the Supervisor version since the compatibility check by the Supervisor is considered sufficient. title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.2 Integrity > 4.2.2 Software Integrity:
text_content: During the design and development phases of the lifecycle for the application software within Varian medical devices, the integrity of the system build is ensured by controlling the editing process and requiring regular reviews of the code. This is done to ensure that the integrity of the application software is maintained through the construction phase. W hen the software package is ready for release to the user, Varian manufacturing procedures (DND1902) are used to prevent improper or incompatible versions of the software from being distributed or installed. These procedures include measures to ensure the consistency of data, the absence of viruses or malware, and the inclusion of needed user documentation and labeling. For Varian medical devices, installation procedures and updates are handled by trained Varian Service personnel or local Service representatives. title: 3.0 Definitions
text_content: Access Process: A software-related action involving the data, information, or system structures, which may include, but is not limited to: viewing, modification, duplication, deletion, import/exporting, and operation. Accountability: The application of identification and authentication to assure that the prescribed access process is being done by an authorized user. Application Software: Software designed to help users perform particular tasks or handle particular types of problems, as distinct from software that controls the computer itself.1 Anything that has value to an individual or an organization or a government.1 Asset: Auditability: The degree to which performed/attempted actions can be traced and reviewed for information concerning the action taken. The information may include but is not limited to: details of the user responsible for the action, the time when the action was performed, the location and/or system structure

title: 9. The patient is unloaded. > 2.5.3.1 Control System Safety Infrastructure > 2.5.3.1.3 Version Reporting and Compatibility Check > 2.5.3.1.3.1 Version Reporting
text_content: Subsystem control software is responsible for reporting its own version and all versions of its software components. On top of that, the Nodes report their subnodes software version (if any) and versions of all FPGA of the node; as well as their PCB part identifier, dash number, revision and serial number to the SPV. When receiving a workstation read version command message via the command channel, the SPV collects its own versions and all versions reported by all Nodes, formats them in XML and sends them back to the workstation. All version messages are formatted to major.minor.revision.build. A major change indicates significant new or updated system capabilities; there is no guarantee of backward compatibility of hardware or software. If a minor changes, there are feature changes and interface changes, and software compatibility could be impacted. A revision is often used for maintenance releases that introduces minor update or bug fixes,

Conversions from Halcyon to Ethos radiotherapy sytem can be made in some countries. Conversions involve changes to the covers, installing and enabling the Ethos TPS (On-couch session manager or OSM) function and changing the nameplate to include “Ethos Radiotherapy System” while manufacturing date and serial number remain the same. text_content: This document is intended to record the product and process standards that may apply to this device.  The product platform Hal 3.0 supports two product models:  Halcyon and Ethos. The completed form presents a list of the standards requiring test reports as evidence of compliance unless otherwise indicated. title: 1. Features and use:
text_content: Introduce a new marketing configuration for Ethos Radiotherapy System V4.0.  There are new EU regulations that require a medical linear accelerator to be IEC 4th edition compliant and while we have one configuration available for Ethos Radiotherapy System, sales is requesting a configuration that is available with version 3.1 but not 4.0.  To address this request, Varian would like to commercialize an Ethos Radiotherapy System with standard imaging as another option to HyperSight imaging.  Halcyon includes a standard imaging offering therefore this change is for Ethos Radiotherapy System only. This new marketing configuration for Ethos Radiotherapy System V4.0 does not change the intended use of the system or any workflows for image acquisition.

title: 1.0 General Summary > 1.1 Device Market History (Continued)
text_content: To better streamline the manufacturing process, the standard imaging configuration for Halcyon will include the existing (43 cm X 43 cm) imager, new kV collimator with bowtie filter, and a larger capacity x-ray tube. To differentiate from high performance imaging, this configuration will be referred to as standard imaging. Also included are new iCBCT improvements that reduces scatter, reduces artifacts due to motion and HU improvements. See section 3.2 for details. The standard imaging hardware configuration applies to Halcyon 4.0 new production systems only and is not available as a field upgrade, however the improvements to iCBCT will be available with upgrades to HAL 4.0 for both Halcyon and Ethos Radiotherapy Systems. · kV iCBCT Reconstruction All systems capable of kV imaging includes new algorithms that will: o Reduce Scatter via Acuros CTS o Reduce artifacts due to motion o Offer Hounsfield Units (HU) improvements Market History HAL Revision Doc Number 4 D59836

title: 5. Risk Management (Continued)
text_content: There are two high-level categorization of post-market cybersecurity incidents. First, there are cybersecurity incidents that involve the possible exploit of potential Varian product vulnerabilities. Secondly, there are incidents that involve the exploit of vulnerabilities in customer operating environment (non-Varian). Varian has both cybersecurity-specific and non-cybersecurity-specific practices in place to support customers and respond to cybersecurity incidents of both categories in the post market phase. The product security team is involved in both practices. The team acts as an interface to identify incidents where both potential customer environment vulnerabilities and potential Varian product vulnerabilities are at play. title: 3.0 Definitions > 4.3 Availability
text_content: To ensure that the data, information, and information systems for the medical device are accessible and usable in the expected manner when needed, Varian considers several pieces of proper device function: the reliability of hardware/software, the performance of the computing environment, and the operation of the device by the user. W hen disruptions occur because of technical issues with these aspects, it is necessary to provide timely recovery of backup data and device functionality. Loss of data due to a technical failure of the system (e.g., workstation power loss, software crash, or network communication error) during the operation of Varian application software is addressed using local caching and backup storage where applicable. Backup data recovery and management for patient and session data is dependent on the specific product and system requirements, and the user documentation provides further instruction

text_content: In the Instructions for use this operator is referred to as the maintenance technician. Configurator (System Administrator) The configurator typically, an information specialist, is a local, in-clinic resource that is responsible for configuring the Oncology Information System and other and associated software. This resource is trained to a defined level of competency and is provided with limited-service access to the system. The role of configurator is to set up key features and functions, and defines any other additional parameters used by the software such as default values, system preferences, department preferences, user preferences and user rights. Americas: Medical Physicist, Radiation Therapist, IT Specialist APAC: Medical Physicist, Radiation Therapist EMEA: Physicist In the instructions for use this operator is referred to as the configuration administrator. title: 2. “Ethos Radiotherapy System ” > 9.13  Australia & New Zealand (Continued)
text_content: device. Reference: Therapeutic Goods (Medical Devices) Regulations 2002. The addition of a new risk control measure does not change the Intended Purpose. Medsafe (NZ) are required to be notified only if there are changes to the information on the WAND database. Similar to the TGA, this includes information such as GMDN code, manufacturer’s details and the manufacturer’s Intended Purpose. Ethos Radiotherapy System 4.0: A variation request or new registration for Ethos Radiotherapy System 4.0 is not required to be submitted the TGA. Variations are only required when there is a substantial change to the design, intended performance or quality management system of the device. Legislation does not contain a definition for ‘substantial change’, however, at a minimum, changes to any details on the conformity assessment (CA) certificate constitute a substantial change. These include details such as GMDN code, manufacturer’s details and the manufacturer’s Intended Purpose.
